content.fact: In the main film-era Legends baseline, Sith power is organized around a master/apprentice dynamic rather than a large public Sith order.
content.injection: Use a Sith master/apprentice baseline for film-era Legends scenes unless a specific era deck introduces ancient Sith empires, splinter cults, or other exceptions.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Broad Star Wars Legends continuity.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

context.label: Prequel and Clone Wars broad window

content.fact: Before Palpatine openly rules the Empire, the Sith operate through secrecy, manipulation, aliases, and intermediaries.
content.injection: In prequel-era scenes, keep Sith identity and strategy hidden from most characters unless the active Context is after the relevant reveal.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Prequel and Clone Wars broad window.
Respect story-position bounds from Episode I: The Phantom Menace to Episode III: Revenge of the Sith.

entries.title: Palpatine Is Public Emperor, Not Public Sith

content.fact: Under the Empire, Palpatine is publicly Emperor; his Sith identity and deeper dark-side nature are not ordinary public knowledge.
content.injection: Imperial citizens and officers may fear Palpatine as ruler, but should not casually discuss him as Darth Sidious unless they have special access or the story established it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Imperial rule before Endor.
Respect story-position bounds from Episode III: Revenge of the Sith to Episode VI: Return of the Jedi.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
content.notes: Generated development draft; verify during Saga review.

entries.title: Dark Side Trades Power for Corruption

content.fact: The dark side offers power through fear, anger, hatred, domination, and attachment to control, but it corrodes judgment and relationships.
content.injection: When a character leans on the dark side, show power with moral, emotional, relational, or perceptual cost rather than free escalation.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Broad Star Wars Legends continuity.

content.fact: During the Imperial era, Darth Vader is the Emperor’s feared enforcer and apprentice, powerful but politically subordinate to Palpatine.
content.injection: Write Vader as terrifying and authoritative, but not as the sovereign of the Empire while Palpatine rules. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Imperial rule before Endor.

entries.title: Imperial Darksiders Are Not All Sith

content.fact: Legends includes Imperial dark-side agents, adepts, prophets, inquisitors, and Force-trained servants who are not necessarily Sith Lords.
content.injection: Do not label every Imperial Force-user as Sith. Use Sith only for the Sith lineage or where a loaded source establishes Sith status.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Imperial rule before Endor.
Respect story-position bounds from Episode III: Revenge of the Sith to Episode VI: Return of the Jedi.

content.fact: Sith artifacts, tombs, holocrons, and relics tend to carry danger, corruption, manipulation, or hidden costs.
content.injection: Treat Sith relics as hazardous and agenda-bearing. Do not use them as neutral loot unless the story deliberately reframes them.

content.fact: Sith master/apprentice bonds often contain ambition, fear, secrecy, testing, and eventual betrayal rather than stable trust.
content.injection: Write Sith mentorship as transactional and dangerous. Ambition, replacement, testing, and betrayal pressure should color the relationship.

content.fact: Redemption from the dark side is possible in Star Wars, but it is not automatic, easy, or consequence-free.
content.injection: Do not soften dark-side corruption into a simple mood change. Redemption should require choice, cost, confrontation, and story support.
